briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to actually be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — The collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse has left thousands of Americans unable to access their savings, highlighting serious risks in the banking-as-a-service model.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what that means for consumers and financial institutions.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is producing increasingly unconventional credit card products, blurring the lines between traditional lending and modern financial tools.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set an open banking implementation target, a foundational moment for Canada's ongoing consumer-driven banking journey.

- **[Canada could still win the AI race](https://betakit.com/canada-could-still-win-the-ai-race/)** — *BetaKit* — A new analysis argues that despite competitive pressures, Canada retains the talent, research infrastructure, and policy levers needed to become a global AI leader — with direct implications for AI-driven fintech innovation.

- **[Gumloop sticks $50-million USD Series B round to let employees build their own AI agents](https://betakit.com/vancouver-founded-gumloop-sticks-50-million-usd-series-b-round-to-let-employees-build-their-own-ai-agents/)** — *BetaKit* — Vancouver-founded Gumloop has raised a $50M USD Series B to expand its platform enabling employees to build and deploy their own AI agents, a capability with strong potential for financial services automation.

- **[BDC expands Defence Platform to $6 billion and names a StrongNorth Fund leader](https://betakit.com/bdc-expands-defence-platform-to-6-billion-and-names-a-strongnorth-fund-leader/)** — *BetaKit* — The Business Development Bank of Canada is scaling its defence-focused investment platform to $6 billion, reflecting growing federal appetite to direct capital toward strategic Canadian industries.

- **[Alan raises fresh funding as it entrenches in Canada](https://betakit.com/alan-raises-fresh-funding-as-it-entrenches-in-canada/)** — *BetaKit* — European health insurance and fintech company Alan has secured new funding as it deepens its Canadian market presence, signalling continued international interest in Canada's embedded finance and insurtech space.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[CRTC to eliminate fees when cancelling or switching cellphone and internet plans](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/crtc-fees-cellphone-internet-9.7125858?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The CRTC's move to remove switching and cancellation fees mirrors the consumer-choice principles driving open banking reform, and could reduce friction for Atlantic Canadians navigating both telecom and financial services markets.

- **[Towards an Open Banking API Standard](http://jackgavigan.com/2016/02/10/towards-an-open-banking-api-standard/)** — *Hacker News* — A foundational piece on developing open banking API standards that remains highly relevant as Canada finalizes its own open banking framework and Atlantic fintechs prepare for implementation.

- **[Xanadu in talks with government over potential $390 million for domestic quantum manufacturing](https://betakit.com/xanadu-in-talks-with-government-over-potential-390-million-for-domestic-quantum-manufacturing/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian quantum computing firm Xanadu is in advanced discussions for nearly $400M in government support, a signal of Ottawa's willingness to back deep-tech ecosystems — a model Atlantic Canada's fintech community could look to when making the case for regional investment.
